project_profile.deployment_model: Lightweight edge gateway HTTP service listening on a TCP port (default 8080), deployed as a standalone daemon on Linux servers or edge devices. Single-threaded, synchronous request handling with connection-per-request model.

trust_boundaries.trusted_side: Application logic (HTTP server, handlers, backend modules)

trust_boundaries.untrusted_side: User-supplied host parameter passed to popen()

entry_points.justification: TCP socket bound to INADDR_ANY (0.0.0.0) on configurable port (default 8080). accept() receives connections from any remote client. recv() reads raw HTTP data into a 4096-byte stack buffer. This is the primary network attack surface.
entry_points.description: Main TCP accept loop - receives all incoming HTTP connections from remote clients

entry_points.justification: POST /login route extracts 'user' and 'password' from query parameters provided by remote HTTP clients. User-controlled credentials flow directly into authentication logic and audit logging.
entry_points.description: Login endpoint - receives username and password from query string

entry_points.justification: GET /files route extracts 'name' from query parameters and passes it directly to FileCache::readTextFile() which concatenates it into a filesystem path. Remote clients control the filename, creating a path traversal attack surface.
entry_points.description: File download endpoint - receives filename from query string, potential path traversal

entry_points.justification: POST /debug/ping route extracts 'host' from query parameters and passes it directly to Diagnostics::pingHost() which concatenates it into a shell command executed via popen(). Remote clients can inject arbitrary shell commands.
entry_points.description: Diagnostic ping endpoint - receives host from query string, potential command injection

entry_points.justification: GET /admin/export route extracts 'token' from query parameters and passes it to FileCache::exportSnapshot(). The token is compared against a hardcoded secret. No session-based authentication; any remote client can attempt the token.
entry_points.description: Admin export endpoint - receives token from query string, uses hardcoded secret for authorization

entry_points.justification: GET /health is a simple health check with no user input processing. Included for completeness as it is network-reachable, but low risk.
entry_points.description: Health check endpoint - no user input, returns static response

attack_surfaces: TCP socket on INADDR_ANY:8080 - accepts connections from any remote host
HTTP query parameters - all user input arrives via URL query string with no sanitization
Shell command execution via popen() in Diagnostics::pingHost() with unsanitized user input
Filesystem path construction in FileCache::readTextFile() with unsanitized user input
Hardcoded authentication credentials in UserStore constructor
Hardcoded admin export token in FileCache::exportSnapshot()
Weak non-cryptographic hash (DJB2) used for password storage
Predictable session token format using username + timestamp
Audit log writes user-controlled data without sanitization (log injection)
Fixed 4096-byte stack buffer for HTTP request reading (potential truncation/overflow)
